JD SPORTS CANADA

Online Shopping Market Pricing

Benchmark window: Mar 30, 2026 - Apr 26, 2026 · Feed exported: Apr 27, 2026 · 14:38 · Report generated: Apr 27, 2026
Overall Position vs. Market
JD Sports Canada Catalogue — Pricing Split
About this report

This report brings together three Google Merchant Centre exports into a single view of JD Sports Canada's competitive pricing position. Every SKU is compared to its Market Price (the click-weighted median competitor price on Google Shopping) to identify where we're over- or under-priced relative to the market. For ~22% of SKUs Google has also run an AI-powered Discount Price Simulation, modelling how clicks and conversions would grow if we moved to a specific discounted price. Designed for weekly use — filter, search, and click any row for a visual price comparison. Ecomm Price is sourced directly from JD's Google product feed (effective price — sale if active, else regular) for live accuracy.

Dynamic SKU Pricing
83.5% of titles have multiple Market Prices across variants. Always work at Product ID level, not Title level.
A single style — e.g. Jordan 1 Mid SE — may carry 13 Product IDs (different sizes and colourways), each with a distinct competitive market price ranging from $138 to $368. This is how the resale and sneaker market works: size and colourway scarcity drives wildly different demand, and Google's benchmark reflects that. A "Nike Air Force 1 '07" sits at one market price for the common colourway and a very different one for a limited drop — treating them as one product would produce misleading pricing decisions.
Ecomm Price
Current effective price on JD Sports Canada for this Product ID — sale_price if set, else regular price. Sourced directly from the JD product feed.
Market Price
The typical competitor price on Google Shopping — a click-weighted median of what customers actually pay at competing retailers. The hero price for decision-making. Unique per Product ID.
Price Gap %
(Ecomm Price − Market Price) ÷ Market Price. Positive = priced above market (red). Negative = priced below market (green). At market = within ±2%.
Discount Price Simulation
Google's AI simulation of performance at a discounted price — NOT a recommendation. A thought exercise showing predicted click / conversion growth if we ran that discount.
Simulation Confidence
Google's confidence in its own simulation: High, Medium, Low. Based on data volume and signal strength for that product.
Click / Conv. Uplift %
Predicted percentage growth (not multiplier) in clicks and conversions at the simulated discount price. e.g. +198% means clicks nearly triple.

Brand-level split of SKU pricing position against Market Price. The bars show the proportion of each brand's SKUs that sit below, at, or over market. Longer green means the brand is systematically underpriced (margin opportunity); longer red means systematically overpriced (demand risk). Click any brand to jump to its filtered Pricing Opportunity view.
Below Market At Market (±2%) Over Market
Catalogue-wide log of effective price changes between feed snapshots. Changes are detected by comparing each weekly TSV export against the previous one. — changes captured so far. Click any row to jump to the SKU on the Pricing Opportunity tab.

Data notes. Ecomm Price is sourced from JD's Google product feed (TSV export), using the effective price (sale_price when set, else regular price). SKUs marked with a red "S" tag have an active sale_price in the feed. The Price Changes tab tracks effective price movements between feed snapshots. Market Price = Google click-weighted median competitor price, unique per Product ID to reflect size and colourway scarcity. Simulation coverage is approximately 22% of the catalogue; only SKUs with a simulation show uplift columns. Uplift values are percentage growth (Google's native format), not multipliers — e.g. +198% means clicks nearly triple. "Your price" sourced from the Price Benchmarks export (canonical); the Sale Price Suggestions export showed 1,249 price discrepancies, reconciled to the Benchmarks value. 1,249 Google sale-price suggestions reference Product IDs that are not present in the current Price Benchmarks export and are excluded from this view — likely delisted SKUs or variants that fell out of Google's benchmark tracking this window. Single-product elasticity — Google's simulations do not model cannibalisation between our own SKUs, so sum-of-simulations should be treated as a ceiling. Last-click Google Shopping only — Meta and Klaviyo contribution to the same purchases is not visible here.
